studyName: Nationwide Children's Hospital Genetics of Congenital Heart Disease

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes subject's age, race, affection status with respect to a congenital heart defect, and specific diagnosis information regarding the subject's diagnosed congenital heart defect.

variables.name: AFFLICTION_STATUS
variables.description: Whether the subject has been diagnosed with a congenital heart defect

variables.name: PHENOTYPE_ID_1
variables.description: First ID for a subject's specific diagnosed congenital heart defect. A phenotype ID will not be provided for unaffected subjects.

variables.name: PHENOTYPE_ID_2
variables.description: Second ID for a subject's specific diagnosed congenital heart defect. A phenotype ID will not be provided for unaffected subjects.

variables.name: PHENOTYPE_ID_3
variables.description: Third ID for a subject's specific diagnosed congenital heart defect. A phenotype ID will not be provided for unaffected subjects.

variables.name: PHENOTYPE_ID_4
variables.description: Fourth ID for a subject's specific diagnosed congenital heart defect. A phenotype ID will not be provided for unaffected subjects.

variables.name: PHENOTYPE_ID_5
variables.description: Fifth ID for a subject's specific diagnosed congenital heart defect. A phenotype ID will not be provided for unaffected subjects.

variables.name: PHENOTYPE_ID_6
variables.description: Sixth ID for a subject's specific diagnosed congenital heart defect. A phenotype ID will not be provided for unaffected subjects.

variables.name: PHENOTYPE_NOTE
variables.description: Denotes observed phenotype if PHENOTYPE_ID includes 'Other'

tables.description: This pedigree data table contains family relationships in the format of family IDs, subject IDs, father IDs, mother IDs, sex of subjects, and monozygotic twin IDs.

variables.name: MZ_TWIN_ID
variables.description: Twin ID for monozygotic twins and multiples. An MZ_TWIN_ID is not provided for dizygotic twins or multiples.

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes the body site where sample was collected, histological type, analyte type, sequencing center, and genotyping center.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body Site where sample was collected

variables.name: GENOTYPING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted genotyping

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

variables.name: SEQUENCING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted sequencing

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs, consent group information, and sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
